title: Parkville, Missouri

infobox.value: is a city in [Platte County](/wiki/Platte_County,_Missouri "Platte County, Missouri"), [Missouri](/wiki/Missouri "Missouri"), United States, that is part of the [Kansas City Metropolitan Area](/wiki/Kansas_City_Metropolitan_Area "Kansas City Metropolitan Area"). The population was 7,177 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[3]](#cite_note-2020_Census_(City)-3)[[4]](#cite_note-USCensusEst2022-4) Parkville is known for its antique shops, art galleries, and historic downtown. The city is home to [Park University](/wiki/Park_University "Park University"), [English Landing Park](/wiki/English_Landing_Park "English Landing Park"), Platte Landing Park, and the [National Golf Club of Kansas City](/wiki/National_Golf_Club_of_Kansas_City "National Golf Club of Kansas City"). Large neighborhoods in Parkville include: [Riss Lake](/wiki/Riss_Lake "Riss Lake"), The National, Thousand Oaks, Creekside, Parkville Heights, River Hills, Riverchase, The Bluffs, Downtown, and Pinecrest.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

**Parkville** is a city in [Platte County](/wiki/Platte_County,_Missouri "Platte County, Missouri"), [Missouri](/wiki/Missouri "Missouri"), United States, that is part of the [Kansas City Metropolitan Area](/wiki/Kansas_City_Metropolitan_Area "Kansas City Metropolitan Area"). The population was 7,177 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[3]](#cite_note-2020_Census_(City)-3)[[4]](#cite_note-USCensusEst2022-4) Parkville is known for its antique shops, art galleries, and historic downtown. The city is home to [Park University](/wiki/Park_University "Park University"), [English Landing Park](/wiki/English_Landing_Park "English Landing Park"), Platte Landing Park, and the [National Golf Club of Kansas City](/wiki/National_Golf_Club_of_Kansas_City "National Golf Club of Kansas City"). Large neighborhoods in Parkville include: [Riss Lake](/wiki/Riss_Lake "Riss Lake"), The National, Thousand Oaks, Creekside, Parkville Heights, River Hills, Riverchase, The Bluffs, Downtown, and Pinecrest.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Parkville,_Missouri&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

A post office called Parkville was established in 1841,[[7]](#cite_note-po-7) named for settler [George S. Park](/wiki/George_S._Park "George S. Park").[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-Hutawa1842MOmap-9) The town of Parkville was [platted](/wiki/Plat "Plat") in 1844[[10]](#cite_note-10) That post office closed in 1962.[[7]](#cite_note-po-7)

The [Benjamin Banneker School](/wiki/Benjamin_Banneker_School "Benjamin Banneker School"), [Mackay Building](/wiki/Mackay_Building "Mackay Building"), [Charles Smith Scott Memorial Observatory](/wiki/Charles_Smith_Scott_Memorial_Observatory "Charles Smith Scott Memorial Observatory"), [Waddell "A" Truss Bridge](/wiki/Waddell_%22A%22_Truss_Bridge "Waddell \"A\" Truss Bridge"), and [Washington Chapel C.M.E. Church](/wiki/Washington_Chapel_C.M.E._Church "Washington Chapel C.M.E. Church") are listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places").[[11]](#cite_note-nris-11)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Parkville,_Missouri&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the city has a total area of 15.576 square miles (40.34 km2), of which 14.948 square miles (38.72 km2) is land and 0.628 square miles (1.63 km2) is water.[[1]](#cite_note-CenPopGazetteer2023-1) The city is fairly hilly and borders the [Missouri River](/wiki/Missouri_River "Missouri River") and Kansas City, Missouri.

### 2020 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Parkville,_Missouri&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2020 census")]

As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"),[[3]](#cite_note-2020_Census_(City)-3) there were 7,177 people and 2,397 households living in the city (2022 estimate, 8,541[[4]](#cite_note-USCensusEst2022-4)). The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 466 inhabitants per square mile (179.9/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 85.6% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 5.1% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 4.5% [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)"), 1.4% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), and 3.5% from two or more races.

The average household size was 3.43.

The median age in the city was 39.0 years. 27.9% of residents were under the age of 18; 61.1% were from 18 to 64; and 11.0% were 65 years of age or older. The gender makeup of the city was 52% male and 48% female.

The median income for a household in the city was $144,127 and the median income for a family was $174,594. About 0.1% of the population was below the poverty line.

About 71% of the population held a Bachelor's degree or higher and 98% were high school graduates. Households with a computer: 96%; households with broadband Internet access: 94%.

### 2010 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Parkville,_Missouri&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2010 census")]

As of the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census"), there were 5,554 people, 1,974 households, and 1,469 families living in the city. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 375.5 inhabitants per square mile (145.0/km2). There were 2,126 housing units at an average density of 143.7 per square mile (55.5/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 89.5% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 4.0% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.1% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 3.0% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.3% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Race_(U.S._Census) "Race (U.S. Census)"), 0.6% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(U.S._Census) "Race (U.S. Census)"), and 2.5% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 3.7% of the population.

There were 1,974 households, of which 37.4% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 63.6% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 6.6% had a female householder with no husband present, 4.2% had a male householder with no wife present, and 25.6% were non-families. 20.9% of all households were made up of individuals, and 5.8% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.61 and the average family size was 3.05.

The median age in the city was 39.1 years. 25.6% of residents were under the age of 18; 12.7% were between the ages of 18 and 24; 20% were from 25 to 44; 32.6% were from 45 to 64; and 9.1% were 65 years of age or older.

### 2000 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Parkville,_Missouri&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2000 census")]

As of the [2000 census](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census"), there were 4,059 people, 1,510 households, and 1,060 families living in the city. The population density was 586.9 inhabitants per square mile (226.6/km2). There were 1,587 housing units at an average density of 229.5 per square mile (88.6/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 90.37% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 4.71% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.52% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 1.31% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.96% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._Census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)"), 0.81% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 1.33% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 2.27% of the population.

There were 1,510 households, out of which 37.4% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 59.1% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 7.7% had a female householder with no husband present, and 29.8% were non-families. 23.8% of all households were made up of individuals, and 5.6% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.54 and the average family size was 3.04.

In the city, the population was spread out, with 26.4% under the age of 18, 12.4% from 18 to 24, 29.9% from 25 to 44, 25.2% from 45 to 64, and 6.1% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 36 years. For every 100 females, there were 95.0 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 93.5 males.

The median income for a household in the city was $68,600, and the median income for a family was $86,820. Males had a median income of $64,917 versus $31,740 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the city was $33,119. About 5.0% of families and 6.5% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 8.1% of those under age 18 and 3.3% of those age 65 or over.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Parkville,_Missouri&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Education")]

Almost all of Parkville is in the [Park Hill School District](/wiki/Park_Hill_School_District "Park Hill School District"). It operates twelve elementary schools, four middle schools, two high schools and one alternative high school.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

A small part of Parkville is in the [Platte County R-III School District](/wiki/Platte_County_R-III_School_District "Platte County R-III School District").[[14]](#cite_note-PlatteCountySDMap2020-14)

[Metropolitan Community College](/wiki/Metropolitan_Community_College_(Missouri) "Metropolitan Community College (Missouri)") has the Park Hill school district in its taxation area, while the Platte County R-III district is in its service area but not its taxation area.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

[Park University](/wiki/Park_University "Park University"), a private institution, has been in operation at Parkville since 1875.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Parkville,_Missouri&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Bill Grigsby](/wiki/Bill_Grigsby "Bill Grigsby"), American [sportscaster](/wiki/Sportscaster "Sportscaster") and member of the [Missouri Sports Hall of Fame](/wiki/Missouri_Sports_Hall_of_Fame "Missouri Sports Hall of Fame")
* [George S. Park](/wiki/George_S._Park "George S. Park"), [Texas War of Independence](/wiki/Texas_War_of_Independence "Texas War of Independence") hero, and founder of Parkville, [Park University](/wiki/Park_University "Park University"), and [Manhattan, Kansas](/wiki/Manhattan,_Kansas "Manhattan, Kansas")
* [Frank Ringo](/wiki/Frank_Ringo "Frank Ringo"), professional [baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") player from 1880 to 1888
